title: Exploratory Data Analysis for Distribution Understanding
description: A flowchart outlining the key steps and visual tools used to explore and understand the distribution of a dataset in statistical machine learning.
elements: Summary Statistics (Mean, Median, Variance, Skewness, Kurtosis)
relationships: The process starts with the Raw Dataset, which is analyzed using Summary Statistics to get a numerical overview. These statistics inform the creation of visual tools: a Histogram for bin-based frequency, a Box Plot for quartiles and outliers, and a Density Plot for a smooth curve. A Q-Q Plot is used to compare the data's distribution against a theoretical one. The culmination of this analysis is an Identified Distribution, which informs subsequent modeling choices.
